topic: Opus 4.6 multi-agent patterns, Claude Code subagents, Anthropic Agent SDK, and multi-agent orchestration best practices 2026

sources.title: Claude Code Agent Teams — Official Docs
Building Agents with the Claude Agent SDK — Anthropic Blog

summary: Agent Teams is the primary multi-agent framework: team lead + teammates + shared task list + messaging
Subagents (fire-and-forget) and Agent Teams (persistent) use same Task tool — differ in lifecycle
Opus 4.6 is the model; Claude Code is the runtime — no special agent mode in the model
Claude Agent SDK provides programmatic agents for automation, not interactive development
Best practices: 2-5 teammates, clear file boundaries, delegate mode, serial fallback for overlaps
cnogo already implements these patterns: implementer/debugger agents, memory bridge, file conflict detection
Key limitation: no nested agents, no nested teams, one team per session
Opus 4.6 brings 1M context, better tool use, context compaction — all benefit multi-agent workloads
recommendation: Continue with current architecture (Agent Teams + memory bridge). Enhance with heartbeat detection, model selection per task type, and parallelism hints in plan JSON. Defer Agent SDK adoption until CI/CD automation is needed.
